title: Added option to share kernel namespaces in libpod and podman.
body:
See notes for my current thoughts and confusions. Most notably: 
I have had  a decent amount of trouble setting up the pause container. The cyclical dependencies between create config and libpod are turning out to be very difficult to avoid. This patch sets up kernel namespaces based on the first container added to the pod, so it will work in conjunction with the pause container once that is set up.

I'm iffy on where to set up the container namespaces. I added a hacky work around because it feels like runtime_ctr is a good spot for it, but that involves skipping setting up net namespace in createconfig.

I also want to find a better name for pause container/podInfracontainer.

Signed-off-by: haircommander <pehunt@redhat.com>

comments.body: @haircommander I wouldn't even use CreateConfig. Use a fresh OCI generator and fill in the bare minimum required to start the container, we can add more as needed later.
@mheon yeah I started doing that, then got anxious about duplicate code, and moved on to this. I will work on that.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 1a439f9) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

Things left TODO (this will be a changing list):
Improve documentation
Add tests (and refactor to be able to be run in the e2e test suite).

If you want to see my current direction @baude @mheon @rhatdan PTAL
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 26b9b17) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Definitely seems to be going in the right direction now, nice work @haircommander
bot, retest this please
I've updated this PR to be able to handle containers opting out of sharing namespaces, as well as added tests and heavily refactored. PTAL
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 06fafe4) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

I think we're going to need changes to RemoveContainer() to prevent pause containers from being removed without removing the entire pod, and to RemovePod() to always remove the pause container (even if force is not set).
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably ccbaa15) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 92e9d78) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@mheon OK, pods now can be removed without -f if they only have a pause container, and pause containers can't be removed by a normal podman rm. this PR is ALMOST done :)
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 3ef9279) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
bot, add author to whitelist
@baude @rhatdan I fininished implementing @mheon 's suggestions. This pr won't be ready until #1266 lands but it's pretty much done. PTAL

@baude @jwhonce updated pod create in varlink. PTAL
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably bf741b3) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat @umohnani8 @vrothberg this should be ready for review/merge, PTAL.
Tests green and comments addressed. PTAL :D

They definitely do not share a PID namespace by default in Kube - you'd
need a proper init system in your pause container in that case, not just a
pause binary

On Wed, Aug 22, 2018, 15:56 Peter Hunt <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> *@haircommander* commented on this pull request.
> ------------------------------
>
> In cmd/podman/pod_create.go
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1187#discussion_r212087190>:
>
> > @@ -11,6 +12,11 @@ import (
>  	"github.com/urfave/cli"
>  )
>
> +var (
> +	// CRI-O default kernel namespaces
> +	DefaultKernelNamespaces = "ipc,net,pid"
>
> http://cri-o.io/ Mentions "Pods are a kubernetes concept consisting of
> one or more containers sharing the same IPC, NET and PID namespaces and
> living in the same cgroup." Totally open to leaving it as ipc,net,uts but
> thought I'd throw it out there @rhatdan <https://github.com/rhatdan>
> @mheon <https://github.com/mheon>
>
